---
title: "EnGens conformational clustering"
author: "Australian-Protein-Design-Initiative/nf-binder-design"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    echo: false
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: false
    code-summary: "Show code"
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| label: parameters
# Parameters are passed from the ENGENS Nextflow process via environment
# variables (robust across Quarto versions - no papermill parameter cell needed).
import os

input_dir = os.environ.get("ENGENS_INPUT_DIR", "structures")
target_id = os.environ.get("ENGENS_TARGET_ID", "target")
dimred = os.environ.get("ENGENS_DIMRED", "umap").strip().lower()
clustering = [c.strip().lower() for c in os.environ.get("ENGENS_CLUSTERING", "hdbscan").split(",") if c.strip()]
min_structures = int(os.environ.get("ENGENS_MIN_STRUCTURES", "3"))
max_clusters = int(os.environ.get("ENGENS_MAX_CLUSTERS", "10"))
gmm_ic = os.environ.get("ENGENS_GMM_IC", "aic").strip().lower()
seed_env = os.environ.get("ENGENS_SEED", "").strip()
seed = int(seed_env) if seed_env else None
conformations_root = os.environ.get("ENGENS_OUTDIR", "engens_out")
```

```{python}
#| label: setup
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")  # headless; figures are saved to PNG and embedded explicitly

import base64
import sys
import shutil
import logging
import warnings
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout, redirect_stderr
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import mdtraj as md
from Bio.PDB import MMCIFParser, PDBIO
from IPython.display import HTML, Image, Markdown, display

# PyEMMA 2.5 still reads mdtraj.version.version; mdtraj >=1.10 dropped that
# attribute (version is now mdtraj.__version__).
if not hasattr(md, "version"):
    import types
    md.version = types.SimpleNamespace(version=getattr(md, "__version__", "0.0"))

logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO, format="%(levelname)s: %(message)s", stream=sys.stderr)
log = logging.getLogger("engens")
# Quash PyEMMA's noisy residue-pair / progress warnings in the HTML report.
logging.getLogger("pyemma").setLevel(logging.ERROR)
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", module=r"pyemma(\.|$)")

if seed is not None:
    np.random.seed(seed)

plots_dir = Path("plots")
plots_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

_MOLSTAR_ASSETS_EMITTED = False
_MOLSTAR_JS_URL = "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/molstar@4.4.1/build/viewer/molstar.js"
_MOLSTAR_CSS_URL = "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/molstar@4.4.1/build/viewer/molstar.css"
_MOLSTAR_JS_INLINE: str | None = None
_MOLSTAR_CSS_INLINE: str | None = None

PREDICTOR_COLORS = {
    "af2": "#1f77b4",
    "boltz": "#ff7f0e",
    "rf3": "#2ca02c",
    "protenix": "#d62728",
    "other": "#555555",
}
CLUSTER_COLORS = [
    "#e41a1c", "#377eb8", "#4daf4a", "#984ea3",
    "#ff7f00", "#a65628", "#f781bf", "#999999",
]

def predictor_from_label(label: str) -> str:
    low = label.lower()
    # Match prefix at start or after a '_' (e.g. rep_cluster_0_protenix_...).
    for prefix in ("af2", "boltz", "rf3", "protenix"):
        if (
            low.startswith(f"{prefix}_")
            or low.startswith(f"{prefix}-")
            or f"_{prefix}_" in low
            or f"-{prefix}-" in low
        ):
            return prefix
    return "other"

def show_png(path: Path, caption: str | None = None) -> None:
    """Embed a saved matplotlib PNG in the Quarto HTML (Agg does not auto-display)."""
    if caption:
        display(Markdown(f"**{caption}**"))
    display(Image(filename=str(path)))

def quiet_call(fn, *args, **kwargs):
    """Run EnGens/PyEMMA helpers without dumping chatter into the HTML report."""
    with redirect_stdout(io.StringIO()), redirect_stderr(io.StringIO()):
        return fn(*args, **kwargs)

def redraw_n_select_plot(cm, method: str, n_sel: int, plot_path: Path) -> None:
    """Redraw AIC/elbow with a vertical line at the automatically chosen n."""
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6.5, 4.5))
    means = np.asarray(cm.metric_vals["mean"], dtype=float)
    stds = np.asarray(cm.metric_vals["std"], dtype=float)
    if method == "gmm":
        xs = [int(p["n_components"]) for p in cm.params]
        ax.plot(xs, means, "bx-")
        if cm.n_rep > 1:
            ax.fill_between(xs, means - stds, means + stds, facecolor="blue", alpha=0.4)
        ax.set_xlabel("number of components")
        ax.set_ylabel(str(cm.metric))
        ax.set_title("Information criterion vs number of components")
    else:
        xs = [int(p.get("n_clusters", i + 2)) for i, p in enumerate(cm.params)]
        ax.plot(xs, means, "bx-")
        if cm.n_rep > 1:
            ax.fill_between(xs, means - stds, means + stds, facecolor="blue", alpha=0.4)
        ax.set_xlabel("number of clusters")
        ax.set_ylabel(str(cm.metric))
        ax.set_title("Elbow method vs number of clusters")
    ax.axvline(
        n_sel, color="red", linestyle="--", linewidth=1.5,
        label=f"chosen n = {n_sel}",
    )
    ax.legend(loc="best")
    fig.tight_layout()
    fig.savefig(plot_path, dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close(fig)

def _fetch_molstar_assets() -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Download Mol* JS/CSS at render time so the HTML does not depend on a CDN."""
    global _MOLSTAR_JS_INLINE, _MOLSTAR_CSS_INLINE
    if _MOLSTAR_JS_INLINE is not None and _MOLSTAR_CSS_INLINE is not None:
        return _MOLSTAR_JS_INLINE, _MOLSTAR_CSS_INLINE
    import urllib.request

    def _get(url: str) -> str:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=120) as resp:
            return resp.read().decode("utf-8")

    js = _get(_MOLSTAR_JS_URL).replace("</script>", "<\\/script>")
    css = _get(_MOLSTAR_CSS_URL)
    _MOLSTAR_JS_INLINE = js
    _MOLSTAR_CSS_INLINE = css
    log.info("Inlined Mol* viewer assets (%d bytes JS, %d bytes CSS)", len(js), len(css))
    return js, css

def plot_umap_by_predictor(
    coords: np.ndarray,
    labels: list[str],
    out_path: Path,
    title: str,
) -> None:
    """Scatter UMAP coords coloured by fold predictor prefix in the filename."""
    preds = [predictor_from_label(lab) for lab in labels]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.0, 5.5))
    for pred in ("af2", "boltz", "rf3", "protenix", "other"):
        idx = [i for i, p in enumerate(preds) if p == pred]
        if not idx:
            continue
        pts = coords[idx]
        ax.scatter(
            pts[:, 0], pts[:, 1],
            c=PREDICTOR_COLORS[pred],
            label=f"{pred} (n={len(idx)})",
            s=36, alpha=0.85, edgecolors="k", linewidths=0.35, zorder=2,
        )
    ax.set_xlabel("UMAP-1")
    ax.set_ylabel("UMAP-2")
    ax.set_title(title)
    ax.legend(frameon=True, loc="best")
    fig.tight_layout()
    fig.savefig(out_path, dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close(fig)

def _structure_to_pdb(path: Path, out_pdb: Path) -> Path:
    """Ensure a PDB on disk (BioPython for CIF — mdtraj CIF→PDB can break ATOM serials)."""
    out_pdb.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    if path.suffix.lower() == ".pdb":
        if path.resolve() != out_pdb.resolve():
            shutil.copyfile(path.resolve(), out_pdb)
        return out_pdb
    parser = MMCIFParser(QUIET=True)
    structure = parser.get_structure(path.stem, str(path))
    io = PDBIO()
    io.set_structure(structure)
    io.save(str(out_pdb))
    return out_pdb

def _load_mdtraj(path: Path) -> md.Trajectory:
    if path.suffix.lower() == ".pdb":
        return md.load(str(path))
    tmp = Path("pdb_inputs") / f"_overlay_{path.stem}.pdb"
    return md.load(str(_structure_to_pdb(path, tmp)))

def superpose_structures(paths: list[Path], out_dir: Path) -> list[Path]:
    """Rigid-body superpose each structure onto the first (CA atoms) and write PDBs."""
    out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    # Normalise to PDB first so mdtraj save_pdb does not choke on CIF metadata.
    pdb_paths = [
        _structure_to_pdb(p, out_dir / f"_src_{i}_{p.stem}.pdb")
        for i, p in enumerate(paths)
    ]
    trajs = [md.load(str(p)) for p in pdb_paths]
    ref = trajs[0]
    ref_ca = ref.top.select("name CA")
    written: list[Path] = []
    for i, (path, traj) in enumerate(zip(paths, trajs)):
        ca = traj.top.select("name CA")
        n = min(int(ref_ca.size), int(ca.size))
        if n >= 3:
            traj.superpose(ref, atom_indices=ca[:n], ref_atom_indices=ref_ca[:n])
        else:
            log.warning("Too few CA atoms to superpose %s - leaving unaligned", path.name)
        out = out_dir / f"aligned_cluster_{i}_{path.stem}.pdb"
        # force_overwrite: re-runs in the same work dir
        traj.save_pdb(str(out), force_overwrite=True)
        written.append(out)
    return written

def show_structures_overlay(
    entries: list[tuple[str, Path, str]],
    title: str,
    viewer_key: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    """One Mol* viewer with all structures overlaid, each a uniform colour.

    Structure coordinates are base64-inlined into the HTML (not loaded from
    paths when the report is opened). Mol* JS/CSS are also inlined at render
    time so clusters.html is self-contained offline.
    entries: (legend_label, path, css_hex_color) — paths should already be
    superimposed. Uses Mol* plugin builders (not ipymolstar widgets).
    viewer_key: optional disambiguator so multiple overlays in one report get
    unique DOM ids (e.g. featurizer index).
    """
    global _MOLSTAR_ASSETS_EMITTED

    if not entries:
        print("No structures to overlay.")
        return

    # Quarto HTML often includes RequireJS. Mol*'s UMD build then hits
    # "Mismatched anonymous define() module" unless AMD define is hidden.
    _amd_off = (
        "<script>"
        "window.__nfbd_define=window.define;window.define=undefined;"
        "</script>\n"
    )
    _amd_on = (
        "<script>"
        "window.define=window.__nfbd_define;delete window.__nfbd_define;"
        "</script>\n"
    )

    assets = ""
    if not _MOLSTAR_ASSETS_EMITTED:
        try:
            js, css = _fetch_molstar_assets()
            assets = (
                f"<style>\n{css}\n</style>\n"
                + _amd_off
                + f"<script>\n{js}\n</script>\n"
                + _amd_on
            )
            _MOLSTAR_ASSETS_EMITTED = True
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
            log.warning(
                "Could not download Mol* assets for inlining (%s); "
                "falling back to CDN links (report will need network to view).",
                exc,
            )
            assets = (
                f'<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="{_MOLSTAR_CSS_URL}"/>'
                + _amd_off
                + f'<script src="{_MOLSTAR_JS_URL}"></script>\n'
                + _amd_on
            )
            _MOLSTAR_ASSETS_EMITTED = True

    uid_src = (viewer_key or "",) + tuple(e[0] for e in entries)
    uid = f"molstar_overlay_{abs(hash(uid_src)) % 10**12}"
    structs_js = []
    legend_bits = []
    for label, path, color in entries:
        # Inline structure bytes so the published HTML does not need the PDB/CIF
        # files alongside it (self-contained clusters.html).
        payload = base64.b64encode(path.read_bytes()).decode("ascii")
        # Mol* Color wants 0xRRGGBB int
        hex_int = int(color.lstrip("#"), 16)
        structs_js.append(
            "{"
            f'label:{label!r},color:{hex_int},'
            f'b64:{payload!r}'
            "}"
        )
        legend_bits.append(
            f'<span style="display:inline-block;margin:0 0.75rem 0.35rem 0;">'
            f'<span style="display:inline-block;width:0.85em;height:0.85em;'
            f'background:{color};border:1px solid #333;margin-right:0.3em;'
            f'vertical-align:middle;"></span>{label}</span>'
        )

    # Do NOT put Mol* JS inside an f-string — its braces would be interpolated.
    boot_js = """
  (function() {
    var el = document.getElementById(__UID__);
    var structs = __STRUCTS__;
    function fail(msg) {
      el.innerHTML = '<p style="padding:1rem;color:#666;font-family:sans-serif;">' + msg + '</p>';
    }
    function b64ToUint8(b64) {
      var raw = atob(b64);
      var data = new Uint8Array(raw.length);
      for (var i = 0; i < raw.length; i++) data[i] = raw.charCodeAt(i);
      return data;
    }
    function colorTheme(plugin, structure, colorInt) {
      var Color = (molstar.Color || (molstar.Util && molstar.Util.Color));
      var value = Color ? Color(colorInt) : colorInt;
      var comps = structure.components || [];
      return Promise.all(comps.map(function(c) {
        return plugin.managers.structure.component.updateRepresentationsTheme([c], {
          color: 'uniform',
          colorParams: { value: value }
        });
      }));
    }
    function addOne(plugin, item) {
      var data = b64ToUint8(item.b64);
      var text = new TextDecoder('utf-8').decode(data);
      return plugin.builders.data.rawData({ data: text, label: item.label })
        .then(function(dataRef) {
          return plugin.builders.structure.parseTrajectory(dataRef, 'pdb');
        })
        .then(function(traj) {
          return plugin.builders.structure.hierarchy.applyPreset(traj, 'default');
        })
        .then(function() {
          var structures = plugin.managers.structure.hierarchy.current.structures;
          var last = structures[structures.length - 1];
          return colorTheme(plugin, last, item.color);
        });
    }
    function boot() {
      if (typeof molstar === "undefined" || !molstar.Viewer) return false;
      molstar.Viewer.create(__UID__, {
        layoutIsExpanded: false,
        layoutShowControls: false,
        layoutShowRemoteState: false,
        layoutShowSequence: true,
        layoutShowLog: false,
        layoutShowLeftPanel: true,
        viewportShowExpand: true,
        viewportShowSelectionMode: false,
        viewportShowAnimation: false
      }).then(function(viewer) {
        var plugin = viewer.plugin;
        var chain = Promise.resolve();
        structs.forEach(function(item) {
          chain = chain.then(function() { return addOne(plugin, item); });
        });
        return chain.then(function() {
          try { plugin.managers.camera.focusScene(); } catch (e) {}
        });
      }).catch(function(err) {
        fail("Mol* overlay failed to initialise.");
        console.warn(err);
      });
      return true;
    }
    if (boot()) return;
    var tries = 0;
    var timer = setInterval(function() {
      tries += 1;
      if (boot() || tries > 50) {
        clearInterval(timer);
        if (tries > 50 && (typeof molstar === "undefined" || !molstar.Viewer)) {
          fail("Mol* viewer script did not initialise.");
        }
      }
    }, 100);
  })();
"""
    boot_js = boot_js.replace("__UID__", repr(uid)).replace(
        "__STRUCTS__", "[" + ",".join(structs_js) + "]"
    )

    html = (
        assets
        + '<div style="margin:0.5rem 0 1.25rem 0;">\n'
        + '  <div style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:0.9rem;margin-bottom:0.4rem;">\n'
        + "    " + "".join(legend_bits) + "\n"
        + "  </div>\n"
        + f'  <div id="{uid}" style="width:100%;height:520px;position:relative;'
        + 'border:1px solid #ddd;background:#fafafa;">\n'
        + '    <p style="padding:1rem;color:#666;font-family:sans-serif;">\n'
        + "      Loading Mol* overlay…\n"
        + "    </p>\n"
        + "  </div>\n"
        + "  <script>\n"
        + boot_js
        + "  </script>\n"
        + "</div>\n"
    )
    if title:
        display(Markdown(f"**{title}**"))
    display(HTML(html))

# EnGens is imported lazily (after we have >= min_structures) so the report
# still renders a clear message on tiny ensembles even if an import is heavy.
```

# Overview

This report clusters a predicted structural ensemble with
[EnGens](https://github.com/KavrakiLab/EnGens): structures are featurized,
projected with UMAP, clustered (Gaussian mixture model by default), and a
representative conformation is taken from each cluster.

```{python}
#| label: overview-params
#| output: asis
_has_auto_n = any(c in ("gmm", "km") for c in clustering)
_clust_crit = (
    gmm_ic.upper() if "gmm" in clustering and "km" not in clustering
    else "elbow" if "km" in clustering and "gmm" not in clustering
    else f"{gmm_ic.upper()}/elbow"
) if _has_auto_n else "density (HDBSCAN)"
_crit_note = (
    f"(cluster count chosen automatically via {_clust_crit})"
    if _has_auto_n
    else "(HDBSCAN chooses cluster count from density; noise labelled -1)"
)
print(
    f"Target: `{target_id}`  \n"
    f"Dimensionality reduction: **{dimred.upper()}**  \n"
    f"Clustering: **{', '.join(c.upper() for c in clustering)}** "
    f"{_crit_note}  \n"
    f"Featurizers: EnGens defaults "
    f"(0: residue_mindist, 1: backbone_torsions, "
    f"2: backbone_torsions & residue_mindist)\n"
)
```

Analyses below are repeated for each default featurizer. Representative
structures are written to
`clustering/<featurizer>/<method>/conformations/` (full original predicted
structure closest to each cluster centre).

```{python}
#| label: gather-structures
# Collect the staged predictions. Anything ending .cif is converted to .pdb via
# BioPython so mdtraj + downstream EnGens can read it. A backbone-only
# (N, CA, C, O) trajectory is then built: predicted structures from different
# engines differ in hydrogens/side-chain detail (e.g. AF2 Amber-relaxed models
# carry hydrogens), so restricting to the protein backbone guarantees a
# consistent atom count across the whole cross-engine ensemble.

def cif_to_pdb(cif_path: Path, out_path: Path) -> bool:
    try:
        parser = MMCIFParser(QUIET=True)
        structure = parser.get_structure(cif_path.stem, str(cif_path))
        io = PDBIO()
        io.set_structure(structure)
        io.save(str(out_path))
        return True
    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - report and skip a bad file
        log.warning("Failed to convert %s to PDB: %s", cif_path.name, exc)
        return False

src = Path(input_dir)
raw = sorted([p for p in src.iterdir() if p.suffix.lower() in (".pdb", ".cif")]) if src.is_dir() else []

pdb_dir = Path("pdb_inputs")
pdb_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

# ordered list of (label, analysis_pdb, original_structure). Analysis uses a
# backbone-trimmed PDB; the original path is what we publish as the cluster
# representative (preserving .cif from fold/predictions/).
structures: list[tuple[str, Path, Path]] = []
for p in raw:
    if p.suffix.lower() == ".cif":
        out = pdb_dir / (p.stem + ".pdb")
        if cif_to_pdb(p, out):
            structures.append((p.stem, out, p))
    else:
        structures.append((p.stem, p, p))

n_input = len(structures)
print(f"Found {n_input} input structures for '{target_id}'.")
```

```{python}
#| label: build-trajectory
enough = n_input >= min_structures
traj = None
kept: list[tuple[str, Path]] = []  # (label, original structure path)

if enough:
    ref = None
    coords = []
    for label, analysis_path, original_path in structures:
        try:
            t = md.load(str(analysis_path))
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
            log.warning("mdtraj could not load %s: %s", analysis_path.name, exc)
            continue
        sel = t.top.select("protein and name N CA C O")
        if sel.size == 0:
            log.warning("No backbone atoms found in %s - skipping", analysis_path.name)
            continue
        t = t.atom_slice(sel)
        if t.n_frames > 1:
            t = t[0]
        if ref is None:
            ref = t
            coords.append(t.xyz[0])
            kept.append((label, original_path))
        else:
            if t.n_atoms != ref.n_atoms:
                log.warning(
                    "Backbone atom count mismatch for %s (%d vs %d) - skipping",
                    analysis_path.name, t.n_atoms, ref.n_atoms,
                )
                continue
            t.superpose(ref)
            coords.append(t.xyz[0])
            kept.append((label, original_path))

    if ref is not None and len(kept) >= min_structures:
        traj = md.Trajectory(np.array(coords), ref.top)
        Path("engens_work").mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        top_file = "engens_work/topology.pdb"
        traj_file = "engens_work/ensemble.xtc"
        ref.save_pdb(top_file)
        traj.save_xtc(traj_file)
        print(f"Built backbone ensemble: {traj.n_frames} frames, {traj.n_atoms} atoms.")
    else:
        enough = False

n_frames = len(kept)
```

```{python}
#| label: guard
#| output: asis
if not enough:
    print(
        f"::: {{.callout-warning}}\n"
        f"## Not enough structures to cluster\n\n"
        f"Only **{n_frames}** usable structures were found for `{target_id}` "
        f"(minimum is **{min_structures}**). Clustering was skipped.\n"
        f":::\n"
    )
```

```{python}
#| label: engen-featurize
engen = None
feat_indices: list[int] = []
feat_names: list[str] = []
feat_slugs: list[str] = []

def feat_slug(name: str) -> str:
    """Filesystem-safe featurizer folder name under clustering/.

    EnGens names use '&' for combined features; publish as a hyphen, e.g.
    backbone_torsions-residue_mindist.
    """
    return name.replace("&", "-").replace(" ", "_")

if enough:
    from engens.core.EnGens import EnGen

    logging.getLogger("pyemma").setLevel(logging.ERROR)
    logging.getLogger("pyemma.coordinates").setLevel(logging.ERROR)

    def _init_engen():
        eg = EnGen(traj_file, top_file, align=False)
        eg.reset_featurizers()
        eg.init_featurizers_default()
        eg.apply_featurizations()
        return eg

    engen = quiet_call(_init_engen)
    # Avoid engen.describe_featurizers() - it builds an ipywidget that Quarto
    # embeds as a broken SVG placeholder in static HTML.
    feat_names = list(getattr(engen, "featurizer_names", []) or [])
    feat_indices = list(range(len(feat_names)))
    feat_slugs = [feat_slug(n) for n in feat_names]
```

# Dimensionality reduction: UMAP

Each structure is represented by its chosen featurizer (`residue_mindist`, `backbone_torsions` and `backbone_torsions+residue_mindist`), then projected into two dimensions with UMAP. 
Points are coloured by the structure predictor inferred from the filename prefix (`af2_`, `boltz_`, `rf3_`, `protenix_`).

```{python}
#| label: dimred-umap
# Free-energy UMAP looks poor on small discrete predicted ensembles; use a
# scatter coloured by structure predictor instead. Run once per featurizer.
umap_by_feat: dict[int, np.ndarray] = {}
kept_labels = [label for label, _ in kept]

if enough:
    from engens.core.DimReduction import UMAPReducer

    # UMAP needs n_neighbors < n_samples.
    n_neighbors = max(2, min(15, n_frames - 1))
    for feat_idx, feat_name in zip(feat_indices, feat_names):
        engen.chosen_feat_index = feat_idx
        reducer = UMAPReducer(
            engen, n_neighbors=n_neighbors, min_dist=0.1, n_components=2
        )
        quiet_call(reducer.apply)
        # Copy: UMAPReducer reuses a fixed memmap path on the next featurizer.
        umap_by_feat[feat_idx] = np.asarray(reducer.transformed_data).copy()
```

```{python}
#| label: dimred-plot
if enough and umap_by_feat:
    for feat_idx, feat_name, slug in zip(feat_indices, feat_names, feat_slugs):
        umap_png = plots_dir / f"{slug}_umap_by_predictor.png"
        plot_umap_by_predictor(
            umap_by_feat[feat_idx],
            kept_labels,
            umap_png,
            f"UMAP by predictor — {target_id} — [{feat_idx}] {feat_name}",
        )
        show_png(umap_png, f"UMAP by predictor — [{feat_idx}] `{feat_name}`")
```

# Cluster-count selection

For GMM / K-means, candidate cluster counts are scored and the optimum is
chosen automatically (Akaike information criterion for GMM; elbow method for
K-means). The red dashed line marks the selected number of components/clusters.
HDBSCAN does not use a fixed-n search (no plot below for that method).

```{python}
#| label: clustering
# EnGens registers GMM/KM as uppercase keys. HDBSCAN is run via sklearn
# directly (not in EnGens' clusterings registry) - CF-random blind-mode analog.
engens_method_key = {"gmm": "GMM", "km": "KM", "hdbscan": "HDBSCAN"}

summary_rows = []
# (feat_idx, method) -> list of (cluster_id, frame, label, src_path)
representatives: dict[tuple[int, str], list[tuple[int, int, str, Path]]] = {}
n_select_plots: dict[tuple[int, str], Path] = {}
cluster_plots: dict[tuple[int, str], Path] = {}
n_selected: dict[tuple[int, str], int] = {}

def choose_n_auto(
    cm,
    method: str,
    candidate_ns: list[int],
) -> int:
    """Auto-select the cluster count. With < 2 candidates the elbow/IC line
    fit is undefined, so fall back to the single (or smallest) candidate."""
    if len(candidate_ns) < 2:
        return candidate_ns[0]
    if method == "gmm":
        best = quiet_call(cm.analyze_ic, gmm_ic, filename=None)
        plt.close("all")
        return int(best["n_components"])
    best = quiet_call(cm.analyze_elbow_method, filename=None)
    plt.close("all")
    return int(best["n_clusters"])

def run_hdbscan(embedding: np.ndarray):
    """Fit HDBSCAN on a UMAP embedding; return (labels, cluster_ids_sorted)."""
    try:
        from sklearn.cluster import HDBSCAN as SkHDBSCAN
        model = SkHDBSCAN(min_cluster_size=max(2, min(5, embedding.shape[0] // 5)))
        labels = model.fit_predict(embedding)
    except Exception:
        try:
            import hdbscan as hdbscan_pkg
            model = hdbscan_pkg.HDBSCAN(min_cluster_size=max(2, min(5, embedding.shape[0] // 5)))
            labels = model.fit_predict(embedding)
        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
            raise RuntimeError(
                "HDBSCAN unavailable: need sklearn>=1.3 (sklearn.cluster.HDBSCAN) "
                f"or the hdbscan package ({exc})"
            ) from exc
    cluster_ids = sorted({int(c) for c in labels if int(c) >= 0})
    return labels, cluster_ids

def hdbscan_medoids(embedding: np.ndarray, labels: np.ndarray, cluster_ids: list[int]):
    """One medoid frame per cluster (nearest to cluster mean in embedding)."""
    medoids = []
    for cid in cluster_ids:
        idxs = np.where(labels == cid)[0]
        pts = embedding[idxs]
        centre = pts.mean(axis=0)
        dists = np.linalg.norm(pts - centre, axis=1)
        medoids.append((cid, int(idxs[int(np.argmin(dists))])))
    return medoids

if enough:
    from engens.core.ClustEn import clusterings

    # At most n_frames-1 clusters (need >= 1 point per cluster with slack).
    upper = max(2, min(max_clusters, n_frames - 1))
    candidate_ns = list(range(2, upper + 1))

    for feat_idx, feat_name, slug in zip(feat_indices, feat_names, feat_slugs):
        engen.chosen_feat_index = feat_idx
        engen.dimred_data = umap_by_feat[feat_idx]
        engen.data = umap_by_feat[feat_idx]
        embedding = np.asarray(umap_by_feat[feat_idx])

        for method in clustering:
            method_key = engens_method_key.get(method, method.upper())
            key = (feat_idx, method)

            if method == "hdbscan":
                try:
                    labels, cluster_ids = run_hdbscan(embedding)
                except RuntimeError as exc:
                    log.warning("%s - skipping HDBSCAN for featurizer %s", exc, feat_name)
                    continue
                if not cluster_ids:
                    log.warning(
                        "HDBSCAN found no clusters (all noise) for [%s] %s - skipping",
                        feat_idx, feat_name,
                    )
                    continue

                cluster_png = plots_dir / f"{slug}_{method}_clusters.png"
                fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 5))
                noise = labels < 0
                if noise.any():
                    ax.scatter(
                        embedding[noise, 0], embedding[noise, 1],
                        c="#bbbbbb", s=18, alpha=0.5, label="noise",
                    )
                for i, cid in enumerate(cluster_ids):
                    mask = labels == cid
                    color = CLUSTER_COLORS[i % len(CLUSTER_COLORS)]
                    ax.scatter(
                        embedding[mask, 0], embedding[mask, 1],
                        c=color, s=28, alpha=0.85, label=f"cluster {cid}",
                    )
                medoids = hdbscan_medoids(embedding, labels, cluster_ids)
                for cid, frame in medoids:
                    ax.scatter(
                        embedding[frame, 0], embedding[frame, 1],
                        c="yellow", edgecolors="black", s=90, zorder=5,
                    )
                ax.set_title(
                    f"HDBSCAN clusters on UMAP - {target_id} - [{feat_idx}] {feat_name}"
                )
                ax.legend(fontsize=8, loc="best")
                fig.tight_layout()
                fig.savefig(cluster_png, dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
                plt.close(fig)

                n_points = max(1, int((labels >= 0).sum()))
                reps = []
                for cid, frame in medoids:
                    label, src_path = kept[int(frame)]
                    weight = float((labels == cid).sum()) / float(n_points)
                    reps.append((cid, int(frame), label, src_path))
                    summary_rows.append({
                        "featurizer_index": feat_idx,
                        "featurizer": feat_name,
                        "method": method_key,
                        "cluster": int(cid),
                        "weight": round(weight, 4),
                        "representative_frame": int(frame),
                        "representative_structure": label,
                    })
                representatives[key] = reps
                cluster_plots[key] = cluster_png
                n_selected[key] = len(cluster_ids)
                continue

            if method_key not in clusterings:
                log.warning(
                    "Unknown clustering method '%s' (EnGens key '%s') - skipping",
                    method, method_key,
                )
                continue

            if method == "gmm":
                cm = clusterings[method_key](engen, type_ic=gmm_ic, n_rep=2)
                params = [{"n_components": i} for i in candidate_ns]
            else:
                cm = clusterings[method_key](engen, n_rep=2)
                params = [{"n_clusters": i} for i in candidate_ns]
            quiet_call(cm.cluster_multiple_params, params)

            n_sel = choose_n_auto(cm, method, candidate_ns)
            cm.choose_n(n_sel)

            n_png = plots_dir / f"{slug}_{method}_n_select.png"
            redraw_n_select_plot(cm, method, n_sel, n_png)

            cluster_png = plots_dir / f"{slug}_{method}_clusters.png"
            quiet_call(cm.choose_conformations, mode="center", filename=None)
            plt.title(
                f"{method_key} clusters on UMAP - {target_id} - [{feat_idx}] {feat_name}"
            )
            plt.savefig(cluster_png, dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
            plt.close("all")

            weights = cm.cluster_weights(cm.chosen_index)
            reps = []
            for i, frame in enumerate(cm.chosen_frames):
                cluster_id = cm.chosen_cluster_ids[i]
                label, src_path = kept[int(frame)]
                reps.append((cluster_id, int(frame), label, src_path))
                summary_rows.append({
                    "featurizer_index": feat_idx,
                    "featurizer": feat_name,
                    "method": method_key,
                    "cluster": int(cluster_id),
                    "weight": round(float(weights[cluster_id]), 4),
                    "representative_frame": int(frame),
                    "representative_structure": label,
                })

            representatives[key] = reps
            n_select_plots[key] = n_png
            cluster_plots[key] = cluster_png
            n_selected[key] = n_sel
```

```{python}
#| label: n-select-plots
if enough and n_select_plots:
    for feat_idx, feat_name in zip(feat_indices, feat_names):
        for method in clustering:
            key = (feat_idx, method)
            if key not in n_select_plots:
                continue
            path = n_select_plots[key]
            n_sel = n_selected.get(key)
            if method == "gmm":
                cap = (
                    f"GMM {gmm_ic.upper()} vs components — "
                    f"[{feat_idx}] `{feat_name}`"
                    + (f" (chosen n = {n_sel})" if n_sel is not None else "")
                )
            elif method == "hdbscan":
                continue
            else:
                cap = (
                    f"KMeans elbow vs clusters — "
                    f"[{feat_idx}] `{feat_name}`"
                    + (f" (chosen n = {n_sel})" if n_sel is not None else "")
                )
            if path.exists():
                show_png(path, cap)
```

# Clustering

Clustering (GMM, K-means, and/or HDBSCAN) assigns each UMAP-projected structure
to a conformational cluster. Points are coloured by cluster membership; for
GMM/K-means, cluster centres are shown in red and chosen representatives in
yellow. HDBSCAN marks noise in grey and medoid representatives in yellow.

```{python}
#| label: cluster-umap-plots
if enough and cluster_plots:
    for feat_idx, feat_name in zip(feat_indices, feat_names):
        for method in clustering:
            key = (feat_idx, method)
            if key not in cluster_plots:
                continue
            method_key = engens_method_key.get(method, method.upper())
            show_png(
                cluster_plots[key],
                f"{method_key} clusters on UMAP — [{feat_idx}] `{feat_name}`",
            )
```

```{python}
#| label: extract-representatives
# Copy originals into clustering/<featurizer>/<method>/conformations/.
rep_paths: dict[tuple[int, str], list[tuple[int, Path]]] = {}
if enough:
    root = Path(conformations_root)
    for (feat_idx, method), reps in representatives.items():
        slug = feat_slugs[feat_idx]
        out_dir = root / "clustering" / slug / method / "conformations"
        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        written: list[tuple[int, Path]] = []
        for cluster_id, frame, label, src_path in reps:
            dest = out_dir / f"rep_cluster_{cluster_id}_{label}{src_path.suffix}"
            # resolve() so staged symlinks become real files under publishDir.
            shutil.copyfile(src_path.resolve(), dest)
            written.append((int(cluster_id), dest))
        rep_paths[(feat_idx, method)] = written
```

# Cluster representatives

Cluster representatives (the structure closest to each cluster centre) are
superimposed on Cα atoms and shown overlaid in one Mol* viewer, each cluster a
different colour. Matching files are written under
`clustering/<featurizer>/<method>/conformations/`.

```{python}
#| label: view-representatives
if enough and rep_paths:
    for feat_idx, feat_name, slug in zip(feat_indices, feat_names, feat_slugs):
        for method in clustering:
            key = (feat_idx, method)
            if key not in rep_paths:
                continue
            method_key = engens_method_key.get(method, method.upper())
            written = rep_paths[key]
            display(Markdown(
                f"### Cluster representatives\n\n"
                f"Featurizer [{feat_idx}] `{feat_name}` ({method_key})."
            ))
            written_sorted = sorted(written, key=lambda x: x[0])
            aligned = superpose_structures(
                [dest for _, dest in written_sorted],
                plots_dir / f"overlay_{slug}_{method}",
            )
            entries = []
            for (cluster_id, dest), aligned_path in zip(written_sorted, aligned):
                color = CLUSTER_COLORS[int(cluster_id) % len(CLUSTER_COLORS)]
                pred = predictor_from_label(dest.stem)
                entries.append(
                    (f"cluster {cluster_id} ({pred})", aligned_path, color)
                )
            show_structures_overlay(
                entries,
                "",
                viewer_key=f"{slug}_{method}",
            )
else:
    print("No representative structures to display.")
```

# Summary

Summary of selected clusters, weights, and representative structure filenames
for each featurizer and clustering method.

```{python}
#| label: summary-table
if enough and summary_rows:
    df = pd.DataFrame(summary_rows).sort_values(
        ["featurizer_index", "method", "cluster"]
    ).reset_index(drop=True)
    df
else:
    print("No clustering summary available.")
```
